plainEnglish: Every input that requires user data needs a clear label or instructions telling people what to enter. Use a real <label> tied to the field, and add format hints (like 'MM/DD/YYYY') where needed. Placeholders alone don't count—they vanish as soon as the user types.
whyItMatters: Screen reader users can't fill in a field that has no associated label, and everyone struggles when required formats (date, password rules) aren't stated.
quickCheck: Confirm every input has a programmatically associated <label> plus any needed format instructions; axe's form-field-multiple-labels flags fields with conflicting labels.
commonMistakes: Using placeholder text as the only label, which disappears once the user types.
No instructions for required formats (date format, password rules).
Multiple <label> elements on one field, confusing its accessible name (axe form-field-multiple-labels).
A label not associated with its input (missing htmlFor/id pairing).

codeExamples.label: Associated label plus a format hint
codeExamples.code: Format: MM/DD/YYYY

codeExamples.label: Placeholder used instead of a label
